reasoning: The vulnerability is confirmed by tracing the full dispatch path. (1) Hook registration: add_action('wp_ajax_order_exporter', [$this, 'ajax_gate']) at class-wc-order-export-admin.php:43 — authenticated users only, no nopriv variant. (2) ajax_gate() at line 512 performs a capability check (view_woocommerce_reports OR cap_export_orders) then branches: if $_REQUEST['tab'] is absent or not a registered tab key (lines 530-538), it instantiates WC_Order_Export_Ajax and calls $method() directly then die()s — the nonce check at lines 546-548 (check_admin_referer) is inside the ELSE branch for tab-dispatch only and is never reached on this path. (3) ajax_export_download_bulk_file() (lines 35-64) contains zero nonce verification; the phpcs:ignore comment at line 68 in get_settings_from_bulk_request() confirms deliberate omission. (4) The legitimate bulk-action redirect at line 597 builds the URL as admin-ajax.php?action=order_exporter&method=export_download_bulk_file&export_bulk_profile=now&ids=... with no nonce parameter, proving the endpoint is designed to work without one. (5) build_and_send_file() is called unconditionally at line 40 — the export executes and the file is sent to the browser. An attacker can lure a victim with shop_manager or administrator role to load this URL cross-origin (e.g. via or fetch from a malicious page) and trigger a full order data export on their behalf. The missing guard is the nonce check in the no-tab branch of ajax_gate() and the absence of any nonce check inside ajax_export_download_bulk_file() itself, unlike ajax_run_one_job() which correctly calls check_ajax_referer() at line 17.
